data.epi_content:
"At least to you.

I didn't want to say these things.

I didn't want to ask for favors either.

I just wanted to joke around and have fun with a smile on my face.

I didn't want to have that kind of relationship where we look down and up at each other.

When favors are exchanged and debts of the heart accumulate, relationships between people naturally become unbalanced.

So I kept putting it off, delaying it, and that's why I never wanted to bring this up, even if it killed me.

"Well, putting that aside, there's something I want to tell you."

"What are you trying to say..."

"Don't worry, it's not a confession or anything.

When the girl I like gets slapped, and I somehow persistently find out and come looking for her, I can't exactly confess in that situation, can I?"

"Then what are you trying to say?"

Well, I'm not quite sure what I'm trying to say either.

I just blurt out raw, unfiltered words straight from my heart.

"Save me."

Even though all my deaths so far were the result of my own choices, it feels like I could say someone has been killing me.

The first time, I stupidly died by letting go of that one murky ray of light amid the repeating horrors and dark future.

The second time, to escape from a repeated life, I borrowed the hands of a young boy to send my head flying toward the sky.

And both methods were excruciatingly painful.

"Huh?"

"Save me, Evan. I'm just really struggling. Ahaha, what am I even saying?"

Without realizing it, I alternate between laughing and crying.

My mouth is smiling, my breathing isn't even rapid, but my tear ducts seem broken as I cry.

Even when I wipe away the tears, they flow so abundantly that I wonder if I might become dehydrated.

My throat isn't choked up, and my nose isn't running.

It's just that my tear ducts seem damaged, and every time I try to speak, salty tears cover my face.

"No, just pretend you didn't hear that and leave."

To that, Evan responds with something unexpected.

"Is there anything I can do to help?"

What does he mean, is there anything he can help with?

Just push me to the back of your mind and live your life like you've always done.

"I don't know."

Your family might struggle a bit, but at least they won't collapse.

If you just reduce the number of armored jesters riding horses, the taxes collected from your territory's people would be enough to sustain yourselves.

I just need someone to listen to me, comfort me, and give me a hug.

Whether it's Vivian or you, anyone.

I miss human warmth. I can't bear being alone anymore.

"I'm curious about something. Can I ask you a question?"

Evan nods with a slightly stiff expression.

Well, some crazy woman refuses to leave the room, suddenly begs to be saved, and laughs while crying.

It must look quite bizarre. If I were him, I'd probably stiffen my face to hide my fear too.

"If you lived a life that repeated every day, and it was unbearably painful, what choice would you make?"

"I'd die."

Evan earnestly answers my nonsensical question.

"What would you do if you couldn't die even when you tried?"

His face hardens as he speaks.

"I'd despair, and then... just go with the flow, I guess."

That's what I'm doing now.

And I've become terrified of dying.
data.epi_content2:
I hate pain terribly, and I've experienced it twice now.

"Right? Relax your face, at least for now... No.

I shouldn't have brought this up. Well, we're not as close as we used to be anyway, right?"

"No. Or are we not?"

"Right, we're not anymore.

You like Vivian, and I'm... well, I'm just an obstacle.

Someone who only causes harm by being around, falling into negative emotions and doing foolish things."

"You asked me to save you."

"Did I?"

Evan stirs magic in the air.

He scribbles something with his finger and creates quite an elaborate pattern.

He takes a gem from his pocket, mutters something, and the pattern enters the gem.

The arrangement of characters inside looks quite captivating.

"Give me your finger. Any finger will do."

I extend my index finger.

As if pointing at Evan.

Evan ties a short magical thread around my finger and transforms it into a round ring.

Then, he attaches the gem he just engraved with the pattern.

"Well, I can't do anything grand like embedding healing magic into objects.

This is my limit. If you press firmly on the gem with your finger, it will break.

Then I'll receive a signal, so... just call me when you want to."

I was going to refuse.

But somehow, this feeling of someone showing concern for me felt too good.

Maybe I just wanted to talk to someone.

I even sat down and talked with that unpleasant Crown Prince without running away.

Even with the smell of tobacco smoke rising beside me.

"Then I can only call you once, right?"

"I'll make you a new one every time you call me."

"You made me something like this before.

Thank you, it's the first gift I've received since entering the Academy."

I show him a smile.

A smile bright enough to be called genuine.

My tears had stopped without me noticing.

"......"

Evan looks at me with a somewhat worried face.

And as if feeling somewhat conflicted, his lips move here and there as if itchy.

"Could you leave now?"

"If you were me, could you leave after hearing something like that?"

"Probably not.

Even if I disliked someone, if they were practically a childhood friend I'd spent a long time with..."

"Yes, we were childhood friends..."

"So when are you going to leave?"

"After you fall asleep."

"I'll say it again, it doesn't look good for a man and woman our age to be in the same room at this hour."

"It doesn't matter."

"If I don't sleep, you'll have to stay here all night, you know?"

"That's a bit burdensome. I guess I'll have to put you to sleep."

If I repeat the exact same actions without changing a single thing, I'll go insane.

So making small changes like this isn't so bad.

I've never tried giving up... well, that would be a lie.
data.epi_content3:
Sometimes my will hits rock bottom and I feel like giving up, but there are also times when I just want to live.

And now is one of those times.

I'm starting to think that I want to escape this unfortunate future as much as possible.

Because I don't want to be in pain anymore.

But no matter how much I think about it, there's nothing I can do on my own.

Nothing except breathing and wasting air, consuming a moderate amount of food.

I have no exceptional talent for magic, no clever mind, no healthy body.

So, I ask for help.

If things continue like this, I feel like I'll die again, just like the previous two times.

Like I might kill myself.

I beg to be saved.

And whether it was in agreement or not, Evan made this ring for me.

I don't know if he can save me, but it feels like he's saying to call him whenever I'm in trouble.

I might die if he doesn't come.

Even if he does come, I might already be dead.

But having this on my hand gives me a slight sense of relief.

"Put me to sleep?"

"Just lie down and go to sleep."

Truly, feeling like he wouldn't leave unless I lay down on the bed and fell asleep, I gulped down all the coffee I had set aside and lay down on the bed.

Come to think of it, Evan could use sleep magic.

My eyelids quickly grew heavy, and I eventually fell asleep.

With a dizzy yet somewhat refreshing feeling.

...............

"My head feels like it's going to split open. Should I smoke opium or something?"

In the past, when friends who ate limes on ships were selling opium to the descendants of word-mongers.

There's a quote written by a friend who ate potatoes and was a fried white person, about the caves that lead to the land of happiness.

In opium dens, one can purchase oblivion. In the cave of fear, the opium den, memories of past sins are replaced by the madness of new sins.

He seemed to have quite negative thoughts about drugs, but I think a bit differently.

Once you smoke it, you'll probably get addicted, but along with happiness, you can also achieve oblivion as mentioned earlier.

And when the money runs out, just...

"Nonsense."

These kinds of pointless thoughts come to me often these days.

The more my body hurts, the more they come.

If I die, there's no way I wouldn't seek it out again.

If I were to smoke anything, it would be marijuana.

They say it's much easier to quit than tobacco.

In fact, I've never tried to quit before becoming like this.

Ah, I probably can't smoke anyway.

This lady has weak lungs and is frail.

Come to think of it, I felt something on my index finger, and when I checked, there was a ring with a red gem embedded in it.

"What was I thinking yesterday..."

I must have been exhausted and not in my right mind.

Maybe I spent all my mental energy crying and didn't have the strength to organize my thoughts.

But what's done is done, so there's no point in dwelling on it.

Anyway, the rumors must have spread by now.

Rumors about the collapse of my family.

That there's no one to reunite them and calm the miners and farmers who are in turmoil.

From what the Crown Prince was saying yesterday, probably... no, I'm half certain.

It must have been the Emperor's doing.

The reason this lady's blood relative, whom she never met after being mixed with him, died is probably because of the Emperor.

But thoughts should remain as thoughts.

Just like how I just thought about opium and immediately pushed it out of my mind.

Even if what I'm thinking is the truth, it's of no use.
